svg.text: Custom skills, autonomous logic, and status decision graphs

g.text: Complex Creations support editable autonomous logic and custom skills like characters.
They enable greater freedom when customizing Creation models and behaviors.
Four Interlinked Systems:
Creation Skill Management
Autonomous Logic Parameters
Status Decision Node Graph
Patrol Settings
Open Entity Placement Interface > Select Complex Creations tab > Choose a specific item
> Click or drag into the Scene to create the Complex Creation.
Same workflow as other entities — the Complex Creations tab contains all available complex creation types.

svg.text: III. Differences Between Complex and Basic Creations

g.text: 1. Basic Information
Transformation:
Complex Creations support scaling with an adjustment rate of 0.5 to 3.0.
Model:
Complex Creations can adjust Preset Status, which affects initial actions and other aspects.
2. Creation Status Decision Node Graph
Configured in Specialized Settings. Complex Creations continuously access it and enter different
autonomous logic statuses based on configured conditions.
References the Creation Status Node Graph where Creations execute actual Skills and tactics.
The Decision Graph determines which Status Node Graph to execute based on priority and conditions.
3. Creation Skill Management
Complex Creations use Custom Skills. Skills must be defined before use in the Status Node Graph.
Edited similarly to Character Skills. Subject to three cooldown timers:
Global Skill CD
Skill CD
Skill Group CD
A Skill can only be used when all three timers are ready.
Skill Basic Settings:
ID — Referenced in the Status Node Graph for calling this Skill.
Referenced Skill — Can only reference Skills that the Complex Creation can use.
CD Configuration:
Initial CD Start Point — In Battle (starts on entering battle) or Out of Battle (starts on exiting).
Initial CD Range (s) — Random time in range; creation starts using skill after effective cooldown.
Default CD Range (s) — Random time in range as Actual Cooldown Time after skill use.
CD Group — Skill can only be used when own CD, Global CD, and Group CD are all ready.
CD Trigger Timing — When Skill ends (start counting after finish) or When Skill starts.
Trigger Global Skill CD — Whether this affects the Global Skill CD.
Ignore Global Skill CD — If checked, ignore Global Skill CD restriction.
Cooldown Time Settings:
Global CD (s) — All Skills for the current Complex Creation are affected by Global Cooldown.
CD Group Configuration (add via [Add CD Group]):
CD Group ID — Referenced in Nodes at runtime.
CD Group Name — Selectable in the specified editor.
CD Range (s) — Cooldown time range for the group. Links cooldowns of multiple Skills.
Skills in the same CD Group can only be used after all group cooldown timers are ready.
4. Autonomous Logic Parameter Settings
Define Autonomous Logic Parameter Templates applied in the Creation Status Node Graph.
These parameters are the basic requirements for combat behavior of Complex Creations.
Initial Effective Template — Active by default at runtime (max 1). Dropdown enum to edit active state.
Template List — All templates for this Complex Creation. "Template_X" where X is the template ID.
a. Basic Settings
Initially Effective — If enabled, template takes effect immediately when Creation is created.
b. Battle Settings — Range Detection
Detection Range — When Hostile Entities are within range, Creation gets their info and enters battle.
Preview Range — View Detection Range in the Editing Interface.
b. Battle Settings — FOV Detection
FOV Type — FOV Cone or Full FOV (updates range editing parameters).
FOV Cone - Cone Distance — Radius distance of the FOV Cone.
Full FOV - Detection Radius — Sphere radius for full field of view.
Chain Battle Distance (m) — Nearby non-battle Creations also enter battle when one engages.
c. Out-of-Battle Settings
Rules for when a Creation automatically leaves battle after engaging a hostile Entity.
Out-of-Battle Distance (m) — Creation leaves battle when distance to target exceeds value.
Leave Battle on Pathfinding Failure — Leaves battle if no valid path to target exists.
Leaving Battle Delay (s) — Delay before leaving battle after pathfinding failure.
d. Territory Settings
Territory — Supports None, Sphere, and Cylinder. Size parameters required.
Centered on spawn location, does not move with the Creation.
Movement range is constrained within Territory (adjustable via Nodes).
Leave Battle When Player Leaves Area — Creation leaves battle when target exits area.
Leaving Battle Delay (s) — Delay after target leaves Territory before leaving battle.
Patrol Settings
Complex Creations also support Patrol Settings (see General Settings documentation).
Configure patrol templates with waypoints, loop types, and patrol start points.
Patrol triggers are configured in the Creation Status Node Graph.

svg.text: V. Managing Behaviors Through Node Graphs

g.text: 1. Creation Status Decision Node Graph
Starts with the "Execute only by sequence" node. Each output pin connects to "Switch to
self execution status" nodes to execute different behaviors.
Execution Priority Logic:
Evaluate node A. If conditions met and executes successfully, skip B and C.
If A fails, evaluate node B. If B fails, continue to branch 2 and evaluate C.
If currently executing C but A's conditions are met, immediately switch to A.
Key Behaviors:
The Decision Node Graph continuously executes. If preceding state conditions are met, the
creation immediately switches execution states to the higher-priority state node graph.
If no conditions are met, the creation may not execute any state node graph.
Failed execution attempts fall through to the next output pin in sequence.
Execute only by sequence (start node)
Switch to self execution status
Bind Autonomous Logic Parameters to each Switch node to define behavior for that status.
2. Creation Status Node Graph
Input and output pins support only single-line connections.
Represents continuous behavior — while a Complex Creation remains in a given execution state
and is not switched by the Decision Node Graph, it continuously executes the configured behavior.
Typical Status Node Graph Structure:
Tactic: Move to Target Entity
then
Execute Skill
If creations cannot attack or show abnormal patrol, rebuild navmesh: Stage Settings > Terrain Navigation > Render Navmesh.
Step-by-step implementation for a Complex Creation with custom skills:
Create Custom Skills
Create Complex Creation > Combat Presets > Creation Custom Skill tab > Add skill with
name and primary model. Configure skill node graph in Miliastra Sandbox (e.g., Trigger
Hitbox at Specific Location for AoE damage). Configure animation in Animation Editor.
Configure Creation Status Node Graph
In Miliastra Sandbox: Create status node graph with Tactic: Move to Target Entity
followed by Execute Skill. Add Skill Triggers to bind created skills.
Configure Autonomous Logic Parameters
In-Game Editor: Add autonomous parameters, set "Initially Effective." Configure
battle settings (detection range, FOV), out-of-battle settings, and territory.
Configure Creation Status Decision Node Graph
In Miliastra Sandbox: Create decision graph with Switch to self execution status
and bind corresponding autonomous logic parameters for each status.
Bind and Playtest
In-Game Editor: Bind the Decision Node Graph to the creation. Place in scene and
enter Test Play to validate behavior. Rebuild navmesh if pathfinding issues occur.
Pathfinding Blocker (Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance)
Add Pathfinding Obstacle component to dynamic objects. Creations will automatically
avoid these during patrol/pathfinding. Place along patrol routes for testing.
Normal Skill
Plays a one-time animation in sequence. Skill effects
triggered at any specified frame within the animation.
Instant Skill
No animation. All skill effects executed immediately
upon casting.
Hitbox Configuration: Select Sector type, configure height/angle/radius. Target faction filter
determines who is hit. DMG = Damage Coefficient x ATK + Damage Increment. Absolute Damage ignores DEF.

a.text: Miliastra Academy Documentation: Complex Creations — Trigger Custom Skills via Status Decision Node Graph

svg.text: AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
